title: Multi trp based scheduling of mixed type traffics
abstract: Methods for scheduling mixed type traffics via multiple Transmission/Reception Points (TRPs) are provided. In embodiments disclosed herein, a base station provides, and a wireless device receives, a scheduling information for scheduling the mixed type traffics with different Quality of Service (QoS) requirements via multiple TRPs. In a non-limiting example, the mixed type traffics include an enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB) service and an Ultra-Reliable and Low Latency Communication (URLLC) traffic service, and the scheduling information can be conveyed in a single Downlink Control Information (DCI) or a pair of DCIs. As such, it is possible to enable efficient scheduling of both eMBB traffic and URLLC traffics simultaneously. As a result, it is possible to satisfy a reliability requirement of the URLLC service without sacrificingspectral efficiency and/or throughput of the eMBB service.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method performed by a wireless device for receiving mixed type traffics from a base station via multiple Transmission/Reception Points, TRPs, comprising: receiving (1100), from one or more of the multiple TRPs, at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising a scheduling information for receiving a first type traffic and a second type traffic from the multiple TRPs; and receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the at least one DCI. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein: the first type traffic corresponds to a first set of Quality-of-Service, QoS, requirements; and the second type traffic corresponds to a second set of QoS requirements different from the first set of QoS requirements. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein: the first set of QoS requirements comprises a Block Error Rate, BLER, target for the first type traffic; and the second set of QoS requirements comprises a BLER target for the second type traffic. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein the first type traffic is different from the second type traffic. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the first type traffic is an enhanced Mobile Broadband, eMBB, traffic and the second type traffic is an Ultra-Reliable and Low Latency Communication, URLLC, traffic. 6. The method of claim 4 or 5, wherein: the first set of QoS requirements correspond to a first of the multiple TRPs; and 

 the second of QoS requirements correspond to a second of the multiple TRPs. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein: receiving (1100) the at least one DCI comprises receiving (1100-1) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the second type traffic from the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH; and receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises receiving (1102-1) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the first DCI and the second DCI. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the second type traffic received from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs each comprises an identical data packet associated with the second type traffic. 9. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein: receiving (1100) the at least one DCI comprises receiving (1100-2) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH; and 

 receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises receiving (1102-2) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the first DCI and the second DCI. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the first type traffic and the second type traffic received from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs each comprises a different data packet associated with the first type traffic and an identical data packet associated with the second type traffic. 11. The method of any of claims 8 to 10, wherein: the first DCI is received in a first Control Resource Set, CORESET, with a first activated Transmission Configuration Information, TCI, state corresponding to the first of the multiple TRPs and scheduling the first PDSCH; and the second DCI is received in a second CORESET with a second activated TCI state corresponding to the second of the multiple TRPs and scheduling the second PDSCH. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the first PDSCH and the second PDSCH are scheduled to be fully overlapped, partially overlapped, or non-overlapped. 13. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein: receiving (1100) the at least one DCI comprises receiving (1100-3) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the first type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in both the first PDSCH and a second PDSCH, respectively; and 

 receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises receiving (1102-3) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the first DCI and the second DCI. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the first type traffic is multiplexed with the second type traffic in the first PDSCH based on one of: a spatial-division multiplexing scheme, a time-division multiplexing scheme, and a frequency-division multiplexing scheme. 15. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein: receiving (1100) the at least one DCI comprises receiving (1100-4) a single DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein the single DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving each of the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs; and receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises receiving (1102-4) the first type traffic and the second type of traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the single DCI. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the single DCI further comprises the scheduling information for receiving from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH and the scheduling information for receiving from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the single DCI further comprises the scheduling information for receiving from the second of the multiple TRPs via a third TB in a third PDSCH. 18. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein: 

 receiving (1100) the at least one DCI comprises receiving (1100-5) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the first type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for receiving the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second PDSCH and/or a third PDSCH, respectively; and receiving (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises receiving (1102-5) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the first DCI and the second DCI. 19. A wireless device (1800), comprising: processing circuitry (1802, 1806) configured to cause the wireless device to: receive (1100), from one or more of multiple TRPs, at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising a scheduling information for receiving a first type traffic and a second type traffic from the multiple TRPs; and receive (1102) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information received in the at least one DCI; and power supply circuitry configured to supply power to the wireless device. 20. The wireless device of claim 19, wherein the processing circuitry (1802, 1806) is further configured to cause the wireless device to perform any of the steps of any of the claims 2 to 18. 21. A method performed by a base station for transmitting mixed type traffics to a wireless device via multiple Transmission/Reception Points, TRPs, comprising: 

 transmitting (1200), to a wireless device via one or more of the multiple TRPs, at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising a scheduling information for transmitting a first type traffic and a second type traffic from the multiple TRPs; and transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the at least one DCI. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein: the first type traffic corresponds to a first set of Quality-of-Service, QoS, requirements; and the second type traffic corresponds to a second set of QoS requirements different from the first set of QoS requirements. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein: the first set of QoS requirements comprises a Block Error Rate, BLER, target for the first type traffic; and the second set of QoS requirements comprises a BLER target for the second type traffic. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the first type traffic is different from the second type traffic. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the first type traffic is an enhanced Mobile Broadband, eMBB, traffic and the second type traffic is an Ultra-Reliable and Low Latency Communication, URLLC, traffic. 26. The method of claim 24 or 25, wherein: the first set of QoS requirements correspond to a first of the multiple TRPs; and the second of QoS requirements correspond to a second of the multiple TRPs. 27. The method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein: 

 transmitting (1200) the at least one DCI comprises transmitting (1200-1) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the second type traffic from the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH; and transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises transmitting (1202-1) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the first DCI and the second DCI. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the second type traffic received from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs each comprises an identical data packet associated with the second type traffic. 29. The method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein: transmitting (1200) the at least one DCI comprises transmitting (1200-2) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH; and transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises transmitting (1202-2) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from 

 the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the first DCI and the second DCI. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the first type traffic and the second type traffic transmitted from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs comprise a different data packet associated with first type traffic and an identical data packet associated with the second type traffic. 31. The method of any of claims 28 to 30, wherein: the first DCI is received in a first Control Resource Set, CORESET, with a first activated Transmission Configuration Information, TCI, state corresponding to the first of the multiple TRPs and scheduling the first PDSCH; and the second DCI is received in a second CORESET with a second activated TCI state corresponding to the second of the multiple TRPs and scheduling the second PDSCH. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the first PDSCH and the second PDSCH are scheduled to be fully overlapped, partially overlapped, or non-overlapped. 33. The method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein: transmitting (1200) the at least one DCI comprises transmitting (1200-3) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the first type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in the first PDSCH and a second PDSCH, respectively; and 

 transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises transmitting (1202-3) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the first DCI and the second DCI. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the first type traffic is multiplexed with the second type traffic in the first PDSCH based on one of: a spatial-division multiplexing scheme, a time-division multiplexing scheme, and a frequency-division multiplexing scheme. 35. The method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein: transmitting (1200) the at least one DCI comprises transmitting (1200-4) a single DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein the single DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting each of the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs; and transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises transmitting (1202-4) the first type traffic and the second type of traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the single DCI. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the single DCI further comprises the scheduling information for transmitting from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH and the scheduling information for transmitting from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second TB in a second PDSCH. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the single DCI further comprises the scheduling information for transmitting from the second of the multiple TRPs via a third TB in a third PDSCH. 38. The method of any of claims 21 to 26, wherein: 

 transmitting (1200) the at least one DCI comprises transmitting (1200-5) a first DCI and a second DCI from the one or more of the multiple TRPs, wherein: the first DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the first type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs via a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises the scheduling information for transmitting the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs via a second PDSCH and a third PDSCH, respectively; and transmitting (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic comprises transmitting (1202-5) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the first of the multiple TRPs and the second of the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the first DCI and the second DCI. 39. A base station (1500), comprising: processing circuitry (1502) configured to cause the base station to: transmit (1200), to a wireless device via one or more of multiple TRPs, at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising a scheduling information for transmitting a first type traffic and a second type traffic from the multiple TRPs; and transmit (1202) the first type traffic and the second type traffic from the multiple TRPs based on the scheduling information transmitted in the at least one DCI; and power supply circuitry configured to supply power to the base station. 40. The base station of claim 39, wherein the processing circuitry (1302) is further configured to cause the base station to perform any of the steps of any of the claims 22 to 38. 41. A method performed by a wireless device for receiving at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising: 

 receiving (1300) a first DCI in a first Control Resource Set, CORESET, associated with a first CORESET Pool Index; and receiving (1302) a second DCI in a second CORESET associated with a second CORESET Pool Index, wherein: the first DCI comprises a scheduling information for receiving a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises a second scheduling information for receiving a second TB in one or more PDSCHs according to a configured reliability scheme. 42. The method of claim 41, further comprising receiving (1304) the first TB and the second TB in accordance with the received first DCI and the second DCI, respectively. 43. The method of claims 41 or 42, where the scheduling information provided by the first DCI contains a single TCI state. 44. The method of claims 41 or 42, where the scheduling information provided by the second DCI contains two TCI states. 45. The method of any of claims 41 to 44, where the configured reliability scheme is one of FDMSchemeA, FDMSchemeB, or TDMSchemeA. 46. The method of any of claims 41 to 44, where the configured reliability scheme is a slot-based repetition scheme. 47. A method performed by a base station for transmitting at least one Downlink Control Information, DCI, comprising: transmitting (1400) a first DCI in a first Control Resource Set, CORESET, associated with a first CORESET Pool Index; and transmitting (1402) a second DCI in a second CORESET associated with a second CORESET Pool Index, wherein: 

 the first DCI comprises a scheduling information for receiving a first Transport Block, TB, in a first Physical Downlink Shared Channel, PDSCH; and the second DCI comprises a second scheduling information for receiving a second TB in one or more PDSCHs according to a configured reliability scheme. 48. The method of claim 47, further comprising transmitting (1404) the first TB and the second TB in accordance with the received first DCI and the second DCI, respectively. 49. The method of claims 47 or 48, where the scheduling information provided by the first DCI contains a single TCI state. 50. The method of claims 47 or 48, where the scheduling information provided by the second DCI contains two TCI states. 51. The method of any of claims 47 to 50, where the configured reliability scheme is one of FDMSchemeA, FDMSchemeB, or TDMSchemeA. 52. The method of any of claims 47 to 50, where the configured reliability scheme is a slot-based repetition scheme. 53. A communication system including a host computer comprising: processing circuitry configured to provide user data; and a communication interface configured to forward user data to a cellular network for transmission to a User Equipment, UE; wherein the UE comprises a radio interface and processing circuitry, the UE's components configured to perform any of the steps of any of the embodiments performed by the wireless device 54. A communication system including a host computer comprising: processing circuitry configured to provide user data; and 

a communication interface configured to forward the user data to a cellular network for transmission to a User Equipment, UE; wherein the cellular network comprises a base station having a radio interface and processing circuitry, the base station's processing circuitry configured to perform any of the steps of any of the embodiments performed by the base station.
